description:
# .43.D — matrix dispatch in lower_multi_clause (eligibility-gated)

## The shape

`lower_pattern_bind` operates on one pattern at a time, so cross-clause
constructor tests (same arity, same atom) get emitted N times instead
of ONCE. For ast_eval, arity-3 is tested twice (clauses 2 + 3 both
`{:_, a, b}`). Each clause projects field 0 fresh. Total redundant
work: O(N²) in worst case.

## The fix

Build a `Matrix` from the clauses, compile to a `Decision` tree via
`pattern_matrix::compile` (.43.C, landed), walk the tree emitting IR.
Per-clause body lowering happens at each Leaf via a callback supplied
by `lower_multi_clause`.

## The eligibility gate (protects existing goldens)

`try_matrix_dispatch` only fires when:

- all clauses have ≥1 column whose pattern is a constructor
  (Tuple, Atom, Int, Bool, Nil, List)
- AT LEAST TWO clauses share the same constructor in some column
- no clause has a guard
- no clause's pattern contains Map or Bitstring at any depth

If the gate rejects, fall through to today's cascade — zero golden
churn for that fn. ast_eval passes the gate; fib_tailrec /
multi_clause / classify_two_clause do not (and stay byte-identical).

## What changes

In `src/ir_lower.rs`:

```rust
fn lower_multi_clause(ctx, fn_def, param_vars, entry) -> Result {
    if try_matrix_dispatch(ctx, fn_def, param_vars, entry)? {
        return Ok(());
    }
    // ... existing cascade body, unchanged ...
}

fn try_matrix_dispatch(ctx, fn_def, param_vars, entry)
    -> Result<bool, LowerError>
{
    if !is_eligible(fn_def) { return Ok(false); }

    // Allocate fail_block (Halt :function_clause).
    // Build Matrix { subjects: param_vars, rows: clauses-as-rows }.
    // Compile via pattern_matrix::compile.
    // Walk Decision tree:
    //   Switch  -> Prim::TypeTest + Term::If (chained for >2 cases)
    //   Project (synthesized when entering arity-case) -> Prim::TupleField
    //   Leaf    -> invoke body_callback(ctx, body_id, leaf_bindings)
    //   PerRow  -> call existing lower_pattern_bind for that row's pat
    //   Fail    -> Term::Goto(fail_block, vec![])
    // body_callback:
    //   env.clear; bind outer params + leaf bindings
    //   if body_might_cps_split(clause.body): cont_fn wrap (today)
    //   else: lower inline + Return (today)
    Ok(true)
}
```

## What stays

- The cascade for ineligible fns (today's lower_multi_clause body).
- `body_might_cps_split` + `mint_cont_fn` + cont_fn dance — invoked
  from inside the body callback, unchanged.
- `emit_param_type_guards` — invoked at the leaf after binding, before
  the cont_fn/inline decision.
- All IR primitives (no Term::Switch in this ticket).

## What's removed

- Per-clause `try_blocks` cascade FOR ELIGIBLE FNS ONLY.
- Cross-clause duplicate arity/atom TypeTests (the structural win).
- Cross-clause duplicate `TupleField` projections (one set per arity
  group, shared).

## Acceptance

- ast_eval expected.clif STRICTLY smaller than current (count
  TypeTests: 2 arity tests, not 3; field-0 projection emitted twice
  not three times).
- All other fixtures: golden byte-identical (cascade fallback).
- Three-path parity (interp/JIT/AOT all pass for every fixture).
- New unit test: `try_matrix_dispatch_fires_for_ast_eval_shape` —
  build a multi-clause fn matching ast_eval shape, verify the
  emitted IR has the expected Switch structure.
- New unit test: `try_matrix_dispatch_rejects_mixed_patterns` —
  fib_tailrec-shaped fn falls through to cascade.

## Risks

**Cont_fn capture timing.** Today `mint_cont_fn` captures env at
post-pattern-bind. Matrix preserves this by calling the body callback
AFTER all bindings are set; mint_cont_fn invoked inside the callback
sees the right env.

**Spec keys for recursive calls.** Projection Vars at leaves have the
same types as today's per-clause projections, so call-site arg descrs
don't change. Predicted: no spec churn beyond what A+B already did.
Verify on bless.

**Eligibility false-positives.** If is_eligible returns true for a fn
where matrix lowering doesn't actually share work (e.g., all clauses
have distinct arities), the matrix produces same-shape IR as cascade
but through more code. Mitigation: gate requires AT LEAST TWO clauses
share a constructor, which is exactly the "sharing is possible" check.

**Var collision.** Matrix emits projection Vars via ctx.let_, which
always produces fresh Vars. No collision risk.

## NOT in this ticket

- Term::Switch IR (deferred — see DEFERRAL note below)
- Multi-column matrix specialization (v1 picks col 0 only)
- Guard support (v1 falls back to cascade when any clause has a guard)
- Map / Bitstring patterns (v1 falls back to cascade if any clause uses
  them at any depth; .H's PerRow handles single-pattern bitstring/map
  via the existing lower_pattern_bind branches)

## DEFERRAL: Term::Switch + br_table

E.1 + E.2 (Term::Switch + br_table codegen) are deferred. Reason:
for typical atom-dispatch widths in fz fixtures (3-5 cases) the
br_table index-translation overhead exceeds chained-If savings. The
structural win in D (sharing tests across clauses) is where the gain
is. Re-file as future ticket once we have a fixture with ≥8-way
dense dispatch that warrants br_table.

comments.text:
Design refinement after .43.C land — read before starting impl.

KEY CONSTRAINT discovered while reading lower_multi_clause:

The cont_fn wrap is per-clause (one fn_clause_N cont fn per body that
might cps-split). The wrap captures env AT the point pattern_bind
completes for that clause. Body lowers either:
  (a) inline in try_block (no cps-split), OR
  (b) in a cont fn (TailCall'd from try_block; cont fn lowers body
      with captured env restored).

When we restructure to matrix-style:
- arity-dispatch happens FIRST (TypeTest, branch into per-arity block)
- projection happens INSIDE per-arity block (TupleField 0, 1, ..)
- atom-dispatch happens AFTER projection (TypeTest/Eq on field 0)
- per-clause leaf: env.clear + bind from projections + body decision

The cont_fn wrap MUST still happen per-clause at leaf time. Each leaf
is essentially the existing try_block end.

IMPLEMENTATION PLAN:

1. Add fn 'try_matrix_dispatch(ctx, fn_def, param_vars, entry, fail_block)
   -> Result<bool, LowerError>'. Returns Ok(true) if the matrix path
   handled it, Ok(false) if fell through to existing cascade.

2. Eligibility check for v1: all clauses have single Pattern::Tuple
   parameter (single-arg fn, tuple destructure). ast_eval qualifies.
   multi_clause / fib_tailrec / classify_two_clause don't (mixed
   literal+wildcard patterns) → they keep cascade.

3. Group clauses by tuple arity. Emit:
   a. Chain of arity TypeTests at entry (one per arity in source order).
      Pass-through: arity 2 test → arity_2_block; on false → arity 3 test
      → arity_3_block; on false → fail_block.
   b. Per arity block: project all fields (TupleField 0..N-1), naming
      result Vars 'field_<col>'. These are shared across clauses in the
      arity group.
   c. Per arity, sub-cascade clauses by atom-at-field-0:
      - If field-0 pattern is Pattern::Atom for all clauses in group:
        TypeTest atom literal per clause, falling through to next
        clause's atom check, ending at fail.
      - Else: fall back to per-clause cascade WITHIN this arity group
        (still saves the arity test).
   d. Per-clause leaf: env.clear + env_order.clear, bind names from the
      projected field Vars (recursive into nested patterns is just
      Var/Wildcard for v1; non-trivial → fall back to per-clause).
      Then body decision (cont_fn vs inline) UNCHANGED.

4. The 'second pass' (body lowering in cont fns) is unchanged from
   current code — clause_conts vector still holds per-clause Option<ContFn>.

5. Re-bless ast_eval. Expected: ONE arity-3 TypeTest instead of TWO
   (saves the second redundant arity test). Plus ONE arity-2 TypeTest.
   Projection of field 0 done ONCE per arity group instead of per
   clause. CLIF strictly smaller.

NON-ELIGIBLE PATHS:

- Multi-param fns: skip v1. Future ticket: multi-column matrix.
- Patterns with nested constructors (e.g. {:add, {:num, _}, _}):
  v1 falls back. Future: recursive matrix specialization (.43.D.2).
- Patterns with guards: v1 falls back. Future: guard-aware leaves.
- List/Map/Bitstring patterns: v1 falls back.

REMOVAL CATALOG for .43.D specifically:
- For eligible fns (ast_eval-shape), per-clause try_blocks are gone.
- For eligible fns, cross-clause arity-test duplication is gone.
- For ineligible fns: no change (cascade preserved).

ACCEPTANCE for v1:
- ast_eval expected.clif strictly smaller (verify post-bless)
- All other fixtures byte-identical (cascade fallback preserves)
- Three-path parity holds
- pattern_matrix's compile() not yet used by lowerer — that's .43.D.2
  if we want full matrix; v1 just borrows the algorithm idea inline.

NEXT SESSION starting point: implement try_matrix_dispatch in
ir_lower.rs ~line 1030, BEFORE the existing cascade body of
lower_multi_clause.

title: fz-ul4.43.D: lower_pattern_matrix + wire into lower_multi_clause
